items.description: No 0.0.0.0/0 ingress rules in any firewall or security group
All managed services accessed via VPC endpoints or private connectivity
No world-readable storage buckets or containers
All secrets stored in secret manager — not in env files, CI logs, or container images
IAM roles follow least privilege — no wildcard permissions or admin roles
Network segmentation reviewed — web, app, and data tiers isolated
WAF rules updated if new public endpoints added
Cloud audit logging confirmed for all new resources
IaC scan passed (Checkov/tfsec/Terrascan) with no HIGH/CRITICAL findings
Container scan passed — no CRITICAL CVEs with available fix
Terraform state stored with encryption and locking — restricted access
Provider and module versions pinned to exact versions — no floating ranges
Drift detection enabled — unauthorized changes trigger alerts
Backups configured and restore tested for all data stores
TLS 1.3 configured — TLS 1.0/1.1 disabled on all endpoints
Encryption at rest with CMEK/KMS for all data stores
MFA enforced for all console and cloud provider access
SBOM generated for all container images included in this change
SLSA provenance attestation generated for release artifacts
Threat model completed and reviewed for this infrastructure change
Zero Trust controls applied — explicit authentication for all service-to-service calls
DDoS protection enabled for public-facing load balancers
IMDSv2 enforced on all EC2/cloud instances (hop limit 1, token required); SSRF to 169.254.169.254 blocked at network layer — prevents cloud metadata credential theft
Egress network filtering restricts outbound connections to known-good destinations; unexpected outbound traffic triggers alerts within 15 minutes
Kubernetes pods run with Restricted security context: runAsNonRoot, readOnlyRootFilesystem, allowPrivilegeEscalation:false, seccomp profile enforced
All secrets and credentials have automated rotation policy with maximum rotation interval documented and enforced; rotation failures trigger immediate alerts
Runtime threat detection enabled (Falco, GuardDuty, Defender for Cloud, or equivalent) on all compute; anomalies generate alerts within 15 minutes with documented runbook

items.title: IMDSv1 disabled — http_tokens=required enforced on all EC2 instances
Lambda function URLs not configured with AuthType NONE
ECR repositories have scan-on-push enabled
ECS tasks do not use host network mode
CloudTrail configured as multi-region trail
S3 server access logging enabled on all sensitive buckets
VPC flow logs enabled
Cross-account IAM trust policies include sts:ExternalId condition
GCP instances do not use the default Compute Engine service account
No GCP project-level SSH keys configured
GCP compute instances without external IPs unless required
Azure resources do not have public_network_access_enabled=true unless required
All databases and stateful resources have deletion_protection=true
VPC endpoints configured for S3/ECR — service traffic does not route over public internet
AWS GuardDuty enabled in all regions
AWS Security Hub enabled for centralized findings aggregation
Kubernetes containers do not run as root (runAsUser: 0)
Container capabilities fully dropped (capabilities.drop: [ALL])
No NodePort services exposing sensitive internal services
Helm v2 Tiller not deployed in cluster
Docker socket not mounted inside any Kubernetes pod
Kubernetes API server anonymous-auth disabled
Istio/Linkerd PeerAuthentication enforces STRICT mTLS mode
All Dockerfiles include USER directive — containers do not run as root
Dockerfiles do not use ADD with remote URLs without integrity verification
No secrets in Dockerfile ENV instructions
No containers started with --privileged or privileged:true
Docker socket not mounted into any container
